implementation_summary: Relabeled block/unblock/blocked-badge/blocked-notice/hidden-filter copy in app/src/lib/i18n.ts for both en and de locales (AC-COPY-1 through AC-COPY-5), and added 4 new confirm-dialog copy keys under the contacts namespace for both locales plus the Copy type (AC-COPY-6): contacts.blockConfirmTitle, contacts.blockConfirmBody, contacts.blockConfirmButton, contacts.blockCancelButton. contacts.blockConfirmBody warns that blocking deletes the conversation history and stops the person from messaging (S4's confirm dialog consumes these four keys). Reconciliation: AC-COPY-1/AC-COPY-2 name copy.contacts.archiveAction/unarchiveAction, but that key pair does not exist under `contacts` and is not consumed anywhere -- the live archive/unarchive button (app/pages/profile.tsx:752) reads copy.profile.archiveAction/copy.profile.unarchiveAction, and contacts.tsx has no archive/unarchive button of its own. Relabeled copy.profile.archiveAction -> 'Block contact'/'Kontakt blockieren' and copy.profile.unarchiveAction -> 'Unblock contact'/'Kontakt entsperren' instead of adding an orphan, unconsumed copy.contacts.archiveAction/unarchiveAction pair. No 'Hide'/'Hidden'/'ausblenden'/'Versteckt' wording remains anywhere in i18n.ts for these surfaces. New confirm-dialog keys for S4 to consume verbatim: copy.contacts.blockConfirmTitle, copy.contacts.blockConfirmBody, copy.contacts.blockConfirmButton, copy.contacts.blockCancelButton (all plain strings, no dynamic interpolation, mirroring the LeaveGroupButton confirm-modal convention of not naming the target inline).

retrospective.harder_than_needed: AC-COPY-1 and AC-COPY-2 name a key path (copy.contacts.archiveAction/unarchiveAction) that has never existed in the codebase -- the actual archive/unarchive toggle lives under copy.profile.* in app/pages/profile.tsx, and contacts.tsx has no archive button at all. Verifying this required cross-referencing the AC text against grep results across both i18n.ts and its two consuming pages before the correct edit target was clear; a spec that had verified the real key path up front would have saved that reconciliation step.
retrospective.surprised_by: The AC's referenced key namespace (contacts.archiveAction/unarchiveAction) does not exist and is not consumed by any component -- the real toggle is under profile.*, which the AC's authors apparently assumed was under contacts.* by analogy with the other four relabeled keys (hiddenBadge, archivedDetailNotice, etc.) that genuinely do live under contacts.
